title: Applied Physics

infobox.value: * [Classical mechanics](/wiki/Classical_mechanics "Classical mechanics")   + [Newtonian](/wiki/Newton%27s_laws_of_motion "Newton's laws of motion")   + [Analytical](/wiki/Analytical_mechanics "Analytical mechanics")   + [Celestial](/wiki/Celestial_mechanics "Celestial mechanics")   + [Continuum](/wiki/Continuum_mechanics "Continuum mechanics") * [Acoustics](/wiki/Acoustics "Acoustics") * [Classical electromagnetism](/wiki/Classical_electromagnetism "Classical electromagnetism") * [Classical optics](/wiki/Classical_optics "Classical optics")   + [Ray](/wiki/Geometrical_optics "Geometrical optics")   + [Wave](/wiki/Physical_optics "Physical optics") * [Thermodynamics](/wiki/Thermodynamics "Thermodynamics")   + [Statistical](/wiki/Statistical_mechanics "Statistical mechanics")   + [Non-equilibrium](/wiki/Non-equilibrium_thermodynamics "Non-equilibrium thermodynamics")

* [Relativistic mechanics](/wiki/Relativistic_mechanics "Relativistic mechanics")   + [Special](/wiki/Special_relativity "Special relativity")   + [General](/wiki/General_relativity "General relativity") * [Nuclear physics](/wiki/Nuclear_physics "Nuclear physics") * [Particle physics](/wiki/Particle_physics "Particle physics") * [Quantum mechanics](/wiki/Quantum_mechanics "Quantum mechanics") * [Atomic, molecular, and optical physics](/wiki/Atomic,_molecular,_and_optical_physics "Atomic, molecular, and optical physics")   + [Atomic](/wiki/Atomic_physics "Atomic physics")   + [Molecular](/wiki/Molecular_physics "Molecular physics")   + [Modern optics](/wiki/Optics#Modern_optics "Optics") * [Condensed matter physics](/wiki/Condensed_matter_physics "Condensed matter physics")   + [Solid-state physics](/wiki/Solid-state_physics "Solid-state physics")   + [Crystallography](/wiki/Crystallography "Crystallography")

* [Astrophysics](/wiki/Astrophysics "Astrophysics") ([outline](/wiki/Outline_of_astrophysics "Outline of astrophysics")) * [Atmospheric physics](/wiki/Atmospheric_physics "Atmospheric physics") * [Biophysics](/wiki/Biophysics "Biophysics") * [Chemical physics](/wiki/Chemical_physics "Chemical physics") * [Geophysics](/wiki/Geophysics "Geophysics") * [Materials science](/wiki/Materials_science "Materials science") * [Mathematical physics](/wiki/Mathematical_physics "Mathematical physics") * [Medical physics](/wiki/Medical_physics "Medical physics") * [Ocean physics](/wiki/Physical_oceanography "Physical oceanography") * [Quantum information science](/wiki/Quantum_information_science "Quantum information science")
* [History of physics](/wiki/History_of_physics "History of physics") * [Nobel Prize in Physics](/wiki/Nobel_Prize_in_Physics "Nobel Prize in Physics") * [Philosophy

is the application of [physics](/wiki/Physics "Physics") to solve scientific or engineering problems. It is usually considered a bridge or a connection between physics and [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering").

["General Information on Applied Physics"](https://web.archive.org/web/20070307121331/http://www.stanford.edu/dept/app-physics/general/). Stanford Department of Applied Physics. Archived from [the original](https://www.stanford.edu/dept/app-physics/general/) on 7 March 2007.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Applied Physics](/w/index.php?title=Applied_Physics&redirect=no "Applied Physics"))

Connection between physics and engineering

For a topical guide, see [Outline of applied physics](/wiki/Outline_of_applied_physics "Outline of applied physics").

For other uses, see [Applied Physics (disambiguation)](/wiki/Applied_Physics_(disambiguation) "Applied Physics (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a0/Military_laser_experiment.jpg/250px-Military_laser_experiment.jpg)](/wiki/File:Military_laser_experiment.jpg)

Experiment using a [laser](/wiki/Laser "Laser")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/36/MRI_head_side.jpg/250px-MRI_head_side.jpg)](/wiki/File:MRI_head_side.jpg)

A [magnetic resonance image](/wiki/Magnetic_resonance_image "Magnetic resonance image")

**Applied physics** is the application of [physics](/wiki/Physics "Physics") to solve scientific or engineering problems. It is usually considered a bridge or a connection between physics and [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering").
"Applied" is distinguished from "pure" by a subtle combination of factors, such as the motivation and attitude of researchers and the nature of the relationship to the [technology](/wiki/Technology "Technology") or [science](/wiki/Science "Science") that may be affected by the work. Applied physics is rooted in the fundamental truths and basic concepts of the [physical sciences](/wiki/Physical_science "Physical science") but is concerned with the utilization of scientific principles in practical devices and systems and with the application of physics in other areas of science and [high technology](/wiki/High_technology "High technology").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/ba/CFD_Shuttle.jpg/250px-CFD_Shuttle.jpg)](/wiki/File:CFD_Shuttle.jpg)

Computer modeling of the [Space Shuttle](/wiki/Space_Shuttle "Space Shuttle") during re-entry

## Examples of research and development areas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Applied_physics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Examples of research and development areas")]

* [Accelerator physics](/wiki/Accelerator_physics "Accelerator physics")
* [Acoustics](/wiki/Acoustics "Acoustics")
* [Atmospheric physics](/wiki/Atmospheric_physics "Atmospheric physics")
* [Biophysics](/wiki/Biophysics "Biophysics")
* [Brain–computer interfacing](/wiki/Brain%E2%80%93computer_interface "Brain–computer interface")
* [Chemical physics](/wiki/Chemical_physics "Chemical physics")
* [Differentiable programming](/wiki/Differentiable_programming "Differentiable programming")
  + [Artificial intelligence](/wiki/Artificial_intelligence "Artificial intelligence")
  + [Scientific computing](/wiki/Scientific_computing "Scientific computing")
* [Engineering physics](/wiki/Engineering_physics "Engineering physics")
  + [Chemical engineering](/wiki/Chemical_engineering "Chemical engineering")
  + [Electrical engineering](/wiki/Electrical_engineering "Electrical engineering")
    - [Electronics engineering](/wiki/Electronics_engineering "Electronics engineering")
      * [Computer science & engineering](/wiki/Computer_science "Computer science")
        + [Artificial intelligence](/wiki/Artificial_intelligence "Artificial intelligence")
          - [Machine learning](/wiki/Machine_learning "Machine learning")
          - [Deep learning](/wiki/Deep_learning "Deep learning")
          - [Reinforcement learning](/wiki/Reinforcement_learning "Reinforcement learning")
    - [Power engineering](/wiki/Power_engineering "Power engineering")
      * [Power electronics](/wiki/Power_electronics "Power electronics")
    - [Control engineering](/wiki/Control_engineering "Control engineering")
  + [Materials science and engineering](/wiki/Materials_science_and_engineering "Materials science and engineering")
    - [Metamaterials](/wiki/Metamaterials "Metamaterials")
    - [Nanotechnology](/wiki/Nanotechnology "Nanotechnology")
    - [Semiconductors](/wiki/Semiconductor "Semiconductor")
    - [Thin films](/wiki/Thin_films "Thin films")
  + [Mechanical engineering](/wiki/Mechanical_engineering "Mechanical engineering")
    - [Aerospace engineering](/wiki/Aerospace_engineering "Aerospace engineering")
      * [Astrodynamics](/wiki/Astrodynamics "Astrodynamics")
      * [Electromagnetic propulsion](/wiki/Electromagnetic_propulsion "Electromagnetic propulsion")
      * [Fluid mechanics](/wiki/Fluid_mechanics "Fluid mechanics")
  + [Military engineering](/wiki/Military_engineering "Military engineering")
    - [Lidar](/wiki/Lidar "Lidar")
    - [Radar](/wiki/Radar "Radar")
    - [Sonar](/wiki/Sonar "Sonar")
    - [Stealth technology](/wiki/Stealth_technology "Stealth technology")
  + [Nuclear engineering](/wiki/Nuclear_engineering "Nuclear engineering")
    - [Fission reactors](/wiki/Fission_reactor "Fission reactor")
    - [Fusion reactors](/wiki/Fusion_reactor "Fusion reactor")
  + [Optical engineering](/wiki/Optical_engineering "Optical engineering")
    - [Photonics](/wiki/Photonics "Photonics")
      * [Cavity optomechanics](/wiki/Cavity_optomechanics "Cavity optomechanics")
      * [Lasers](/wiki/Laser "Laser")
      * [Photonic crystals](/wiki/Photonic_crystal "Photonic crystal")
* [Geophysics](/wiki/Geophysics "Geophysics")
* [Materials physics](/wiki/Materials_physics "Materials physics")
* [Medical physics](/wiki/Medical_physics "Medical physics")
  + [Health physics](/wiki/Health_physics "Health physics")
    - [Radiation dosimetry](/wiki/Radiation_dosimetry "Radiation dosimetry")
